module_signals.finding: Strong primary base: Q3'22 release (S001), 10-Q (S002), and the SEC-filed Nov 9 layoff message (S003) carry all load-bearing financials, segment economics, balance sheet, guidance, and both anchor events; as-of price $111.00 verified (S005, web-corroborated). Only soft spots: transcript is paraphrased (S004, med) and the trailing-P/E uses one unsourced FY2021 EPS component (flagged approximate). Net: small haircut, not a cap.

Cost-panic washout inside an industry-wide ad downturn (Snap +6% worst-ever, Alphabet +6% confirm cyclical macro+ATT+FX, not Meta-specific death), on a still-dominant, profitable core franchise (3.71B MAP, impressions +17% YoY). The dated Nov 9 cost-discipline pivot (first-ever 13%/11,000 layoff, hiring freeze, 'more efficient cost structure than we outlined recently') is a real turnaround catalyst that post-dates the Oct 26 guidance by 14 days. Held to +1, not +2, by the Reality Labs overhang and founder-control governance.

Core ad profit pool is durable and large: Family of Apps Q3 segment operating income $9,336M on 3.71B MAP with network effects and growing engagement; the cost-discipline/layoff is a positive operating-leverage signal. Durability ceiling is capped by operator grade 3: founder-market-fit engineer-founder with a long-tenured complementary team and demonstrated error-ownership, but a HARD governance/control discount (Zuckerberg 56.9% voting power, Nasdaq 'controlled company', founder=chair=CEO; #2 counterweight Sandberg exiting; untested insider CFO). Net +1.

Confirms survivability + cheapness, flags the spend. Net cash ~$31.9B ($41.78B cash+securities vs $9.9B LT debt, no current debt); $21.1B repurchased over 9 months with $17.78B still authorized; 9-mo FCF proxy ~$13B even WITH elevated capex and the RL bleed; trailing P/E ~10.6x (approximate), EV ~$262.5B. Warning side: consolidated operating margin halved 36%->20%, RL loss $(3,672)M/qtr guided to grow, 2023 capex $34-39B + total-expense $96-101B guidance is heavy; the Nov 9 message signals intent to bend that cost curve down. On balance confirms the thesis with a flagged cost warning.

SIZED RISK, NOT A HARD VETO. The genuine kill-paths are (a) Reality Labs as an un-vetoable, founder-controlled money pit ($285M revenue vs $(3,672)M loss/qtr, guided to grow, no governance mechanism to force reallocation), and (b) structural ad impairment from ATT signal loss + share competition rather than pure cycle. But: the core FoA franchise alone earns ~$9.3B/qtr operating income, the consolidated company is still profitable AFTER the full RL drag, the balance sheet is net cash, and RL spend is discretionary/reversible. So RL compresses earnings power and caps position size; it does not threaten survival or destroy the core economics. This is what separates META from a structural-decline value trap. Warning sizes down, does not zero.

At $111 (~$294B cap, EV ~$262.5B, ~10.6x trailing, net cash ~$31.9B), the price embeds deep pessimism on a still-dominant franchise even after haircutting earnings power for the RL loss and the ad cycle. Asymmetry is favorable with survivable downside (net-cash balance sheet, profitable core, no refinancing risk). Supports a tested starter with a defined add-trigger; not a full-size position until cost discipline confirms and the RL drag is capped.

starter_zone: $95-$125 (near and below the as-of $111; the Oct 27 $97.94 multi-year low is a favorable add point)
add_zone: $95-$115 on confirmation that 2023 cost/expense discipline is real (actual expense trajectory bent below the Oct 26 $96-101B guide) AND the Reality Labs operating loss is capped rather than expanding
trim_or_no_chase_zone: >$180-$200 absent confirmed earnings-power recovery (i.e. if it re-rates back toward the pre-decline multiple before the cost turn is proven)
binding_constraint: The Reality Labs operating loss under un-vetoable founder control: it both compresses normalized earnings power and is structurally un-reallocatable by outside owners (Zuckerberg 56.9% voting power, controlled company). This caps conviction and position size below the Core tier; it is NOT a survival/veto issue (net cash, profitable core franchise).
kill_criteria: Core Family-of-Apps operating income / engagement deteriorates structurally (DAP/MAP decline + continued price-per-ad falls), indicating the ad franchise is impaired rather than cyclically soft.
Reality Labs operating loss keeps expanding with capex/opex rising and NO move toward the Nov 9 'more efficient cost structure' promise (cost-discipline pivot proves hollow).
Balance sheet deteriorates from net cash toward leverage and buybacks are cut to fund the spend.
Governance worsens (further entrenchment / no independent check) while the un-vetoable bet scales losses.
